faculty.summary: Fluid dynamics, multi-scale analysis, numerical methods, singularity formation.

faculty.name: Alexei Kitaev
faculty.title: Ronald and Maxine Linde Professor of Theoretical Physics and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Quantum computing, topological quantum matter, quantum information theory.

faculty.name: Victoria Kostina
faculty.title: Professor of EE and CMS
faculty.summary: Information theory, control theory, communications, networking.

faculty.name: Steven Low
faculty.title: Frank J. Gilloon Professor of CMS and EE
faculty.summary: Network optimization, power systems, internet architecture, control theory.

faculty.name: Urmila Mahadev
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of CMS
faculty.summary: Quantum computing, quantum verification, quantum complexity theory.

faculty.name: Matilde Marcolli
faculty.title: Robert F. Christy Professor of Mathematics and CMS
faculty.summary: Noncommutative geometry, number theory, quantum field theory, mathematical physics.

faculty.name: Eric V. Mazumdar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of CMS and Economics
faculty.summary: Multi-agent reinforcement learning, game theory, economics, optimization.

faculty.name: John P. Preskill
faculty.title: Richard P. Feynman Professor of Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Quantum computing, quantum information, topological quantum computation.

faculty.name: Leonard J. Schulman
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Theoretical computer science, information theory, algorithms, coding theory.

faculty.name: Andrew Stuart
faculty.title: Bren Professor of CMS
faculty.summary: Uncertainty quantification, data assimilation, inverse problems, ML for science.

faculty.name: Joel A. Tropp
faculty.title: Steele Family Professor of ACM
faculty.summary: Random matrix theory, convex optimization, signal processing, randomized algorithms.

faculty.name: Christopher Umans
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Computational complexity, algebra, pseudorandomness, matrix multiplication algorithms.

faculty.name: Adam Wierman
faculty.title: Carl F Braun Professor of CMS
faculty.summary: Queuing theory, optimization, energy systems, algorithmic game theory.

faculty.name: Erik Winfree
faculty.title: Professor of CS, CNS, and Bioengineering
faculty.summary: DNA computing, molecular programming, synthetic biology, self-assembly.

faculty.name: Yisong Yue
faculty.title: Professor of CMS
faculty.summary: Machine learning, reinforcement learning, interactive learning, control.

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition + stipend ($50,000/yr from Sept 2026)

faculty.name: Aaron D. Ames
faculty.title: Bren Professor of Mechanical and Civil Engineering and Control and Dynamical Systems
faculty.summary: Nonlinear control, robotics, bipedal locomotion, safety-critical systems, control barrier functions.

faculty.name: Soon-Jo Chung
faculty.title: Bren Professor of Control and Dynamical Systems; JPL Senior Research Scientist
faculty.summary: Autonomous systems, distributed control, spacecraft swarms, aerial robotics, RL for control.

faculty.name: John C. Doyle
faculty.title: Jean-Lou Chameau Professor of Control and Dynamical Systems, EE, and Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Robust control, complex networks, systems biology, multiscale physics.

faculty.name: Richard M. Murray
faculty.title: Thomas E. and Doris Everhart Professor of Control and Dynamical Systems and Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Feedback control, networked control systems, synthetic biology, autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Houman Owhadi
faculty.title: IBM Professor of Applied and Computational Mathematics and Control and Dynamical Systems
faculty.summary: Uncertainty quantification, stochastic PDE, numerical analysis, Gaussian processes. Joint with ACM.

faculty.name: Joel W. Burdick
faculty.title: Richard L. and Dorothy M. Hayman Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Robotics, rehabilitation engineering, neural prosthetics, nonlinear control. Teaches CDS courses.

faculty.name: Yisong Yue
faculty.title: Professor of Computing and Mathematical Sciences
faculty.summary: Machine learning for control, reinforcement learning, interactive learning, robotics.

faculty.name: Eric V. Mazumdar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of CMS and Economics
faculty.summary: Multi-agent reinforcement learning, game theory, optimization. Teaches CDS 110.

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition + stipend ($50,000/yr from Sept 2026)

faculty.name: David Conlon
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in Hungarian-style combinatorics, finding conditions for the appearance of substructures inside larger structures. Proved the first superpolynomial improvement on the Erdos-Szekeres bound for diagonal Ramsey numbers and made significant progress on Sidorenko's conjecture. Recipient of the European Prize in Combinatorics (2011) and Whitehead Prize (2019).

faculty.name: Vesselin Dimitrov
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in number theory and Diophantine geometry. Proved the Schinzel-Zassenhaus conjecture (2019) and jointly resolved the 50-year-old Unbounded Denominators conjecture. Published in Annals of Mathematics on uniform Mordell-Lang for curves. Recipient of the 2026 New Horizons in Mathematics Prize, 2026 Cole Prize, 2025 Salem Prize, and 2025 Fermat Prize.

faculty.name: Matthias Flach
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in arithmetic algebraic geometry, focusing on special values of L-functions and the conjectures of Bloch, Beilinson, Deligne, and Bloch-Kato. Proved the equivariant Tamagawa number conjecture for Dirichlet L-functions. Develops Weil-etale cohomology as a framework for zeta functions of arithmetic schemes, with connections to the Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer conjecture.

faculty.name: Thomas B. Graber
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in algebraic geometry with a focus on moduli spaces, numerical invariants of algebraic varieties, and birational geometry. Known for relative virtual localization techniques (with Ravi Vakil) and recent work on log Gromov-Witten invariants. Presented at the 2006 International Congress of Mathematicians. Serves as Executive Officer for Mathematics.

faculty.name: Sergei Gukov
faculty.title: John D. MacArthur Professor of Theoretical Physics and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of theoretical physics, mathematics, and machine learning. Known for the Gukov-Vafa-Witten superpotential, Gukov-Witten surface operators in geometric Langlands, and Z-hat invariants of 3-manifolds. Recent research applies machine learning to low-dimensional topology including the smooth 4D Poincare conjecture.

faculty.name: Thomas M. Hutchcroft
faculty.title: Gordon and Betty Moore Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory and mathematical physics, specializing in Bernoulli percolation on Cayley graphs, random walks, and uniform spanning forests. Solved fundamental conjectures in percolation theory and proved that every countably infinite group with Kazhdan's property (T) has cost 1. Recipient of the 2024 EMS Prize and 2025 Whitehead Prize.

faculty.name: Philip Isett
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in nonlinear PDEs with focus on fluid dynamics, particularly the Euler and Navier-Stokes equations of incompressible flow. Best known for solving Onsager's conjecture (1949) on anomalous energy dissipation in turbulent flows, published in Annals of Mathematics (2018). Currently investigates endpoint regularity and local energy dissipation in turbulence. 2019 Clay Research Award.

faculty.name: Anton Kapustin
faculty.title: Earle C. Anthony Professor of Theoretical Physics and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of theoretical physics and mathematics. With Edward Witten, reinterpreted the geometric Langlands correspondence in terms of dualities in N=4 supersymmetric gauge theories. Current research includes classification of topological phases of matter, SPT phases, higher-form symmetries, and many-body quantum chaos.

faculty.name: Alexei Kitaev
faculty.title: Ronald and Maxine Linde Professor of Theoretical Physics and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Pioneering researcher in quantum information theory and condensed matter physics. Known for the Kitaev model of topological quantum computation using anyons, the toric code, and fundamental insights into fault-tolerant quantum computation. Bridges theoretical physics and mathematics with deep implications for quantum computing architectures.

faculty.name: Nikolai G. Makarov
faculty.title: Richard Merkin Distinguished Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in complex analysis and its deep connections to probability and mathematical physics. Famous for Makarov's theorem proving harmonic measure on simply connected planar domains has Hausdorff dimension 1. Recent work on mathematical foundations of conformal field theory, Schramm-Loewner evolution, and random normal matrix ensembles. 2020 Rolf Schock Prize laureate.

faculty.name: Elena Mantovan
faculty.title: Taussky-Todd-Lonergan Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in arithmetic geometry focusing on Shimura varieties, p-adic automorphic forms, and the Langlands program. Studies moduli of abelian varieties and p-divisible groups. Constructed p-adic analogues of Maass-Shimura differential operators on unitary Shimura varieties (with Ellen Eischen). Ph.D. from Harvard under Richard Taylor. Serves as Executive Officer for Mathematics.

faculty.name: Matilde Marcolli
faculty.title: Robert F. Christy Professor of Mathematics and Computing and Mathematical Sciences
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of mathematics, theoretical physics, and information theory. Co-authored 'Noncommutative Geometry, Quantum Fields and Motives' with Alain Connes. Research spans Feynman integrals and motives, quantum statistical mechanics, noncommutative cosmology, the spectral action model, and mathematical models of syntactic structures (book with Noam Chomsky, 2025).

faculty.name: Yi Ni
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in low-dimensional topology with focus on 3-manifolds, knot theory, and Heegaard Floer homology. Studies cosmetic surgeries on knots, finite Dehn surgeries, and characterization of right-veering monodromy via knot Floer homology. Coorganizes the Geometry and Topology Seminar and the LA Topology Seminar (LaToP).

faculty.name: Hiroshi Ooguri
faculty.title: Fred Kavli Professor of Theoretical Physics and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in superstring theory, quantum gravity, and quantum field theory. Co-recipient of the inaugural AMS Eisenbud Prize for Mathematics and Physics (with Strominger and Vafa) for connecting black hole entropy to topological string theory. Founding Director of the Walter Burke Institute for Theoretical Physics. Now serves as PMA Division Chair.

faculty.name: Houman Owhadi
faculty.title: IBM Professor of Applied and Computational Mathematics and Control and Dynamical Systems
faculty.summary: Investigates connections between numerical approximation, statistical inference, and learning through a game-theoretic lens. Develops operator-adapted wavelets, numerical homogenization methods, and fast solvers. Research focuses on automating discovery processes at the interface of computation, statistics, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Huy Tuan Pham
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in probabilistic and extremal combinatorics, additive combinatorics, and probability theory. Jointly proved the Kahn-Kalai conjecture (2022), a fundamental result predicting thresholds for properties of random networks. Also proved Talagrand's conjectures on suprema of selector processes. 2026 Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Antoine Song
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in differential geometry and geometric analysis, focusing on optimal shapes and minimal surfaces. Proved Yau's conjecture (2018) showing any closed 3-manifold contains infinitely many smooth immersed minimal surfaces, published in Annals of Mathematics. Recently proved the Huisken-Ilmanen conjecture on stability of Euclidean space for the positive mass theorem. Clay Research Fellow (2019-2024).

faculty.name: Omer Tamuz
faculty.title: Professor of Economics and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of probability theory, dynamical systems, and microeconomic theory. Studies decision-making under uncertainty, information costs, and social learning. Recently proved that the Boltzmann distribution is the only law accurately describing unrelated (uncoupled) systems, linking economic theory to fundamental physics. 2020 Sloan Fellow and NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Yunqing Tang
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in arithmetic geometry and number theory. Jointly resolved the 50-year-old Unbounded Denominators conjecture and proved Ogus' conjecture for a large class of abelian varieties. Made progress on the Grothendieck-Katz p-curvature conjecture. Recipient of the 2026 Cole Prize, 2026 New Horizons Prize, and 2022 SASTRA Ramanujan Prize.

faculty.name: Tony Yue Yu
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of enumerative geometry, non-Archimedean geometry, and mirror symmetry. Pioneered counting curves using non-Archimedean methods. Published landmark Frobenius structure theorem in Annals of Mathematics (2023, with Sean Keel). Established fundamental comparisons between non-Archimedean and logarithmic Gromov-Witten invariants.

faculty.name: Lingfu Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory centered on the KPZ universality class — random growth models describing phenomena from bacterial colonies to traffic jams. Studies the directed landscape, Tracy-Widom fluctuations, and first passage percolation. Proved the directed landscape is uniquely characterized by the KPZ fixed point, advancing the strong KPZ universality conjecture.

faculty.name: James Cummings

faculty.summary: A set theorist working on infinite combinatorics, forcing, and large cardinals.

faculty.name: Irene Fonseca
faculty.title: Kavčić-Moura University Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: A leading researcher in the calculus of variations and partial differential equations, with applications to materials science and imaging.

faculty.name: Alan Frieze
faculty.title: Orion Hoch, S 1952, University Professor of Mathematical Sciences
faculty.summary: A leading researcher in random graphs, probabilistic combinatorics, and the analysis of randomized algorithms.

faculty.name: Rami Grossberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in model theory, particularly classification theory and abstract elementary classes.

faculty.name: Gautam Iyer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in applied mathematics at the interface of PDE and probability, including fluid mixing and stochastic homogenization.

faculty.name: Dmitry Kramkov
faculty.title: Mellon College of Science Professor of Mathematical Finance
faculty.summary: Researches mathematical finance, including arbitrage theory, utility maximization, and equilibrium models.

faculty.name: Martin Larsson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability and mathematical finance, including stochastic processes, polynomial models, and connections to machine learning.

faculty.name: Giovanni Leoni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in the calculus of variations and partial differential equations, including function spaces and free boundary problems.

faculty.name: Po-Shen Loh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in combinatorics, probability, and theoretical computer science, and is known for mathematics outreach and education.

faculty.name: Sergey Nadtochiy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches mathematical finance and probability, including systemic risk, mean-field games, and stochastic PDE.

faculty.name: Wesley Pegden
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in combinatorics and probability, including random structures, the abelian sandpile, and applications such as detecting gerrymandering.

faculty.name: Ernest Schimmerling
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: A set theorist working in inner model theory and large cardinals.

faculty.name: Mykhaylo Shkolnikov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory, including interacting particle systems, McKean-Vlasov equations, and free boundary problems.

faculty.name: Dejan Slepčev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Applied analysis and calculus of variations
faculty.summary: Works in applied analysis and the calculus of variations, including PDE and variational problems arising in data science and machine learning.

faculty.name: Richard Statman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in mathematical logic and theoretical computer science, particularly the lambda calculus and combinatory logic.

faculty.name: Prasad Tetali
faculty.title: Alexander M. Knaster Professor and Department Head
faculty.summary: Works in combinatorics, discrete probability, and optimization, including Markov chain mixing, functional inequalities, and randomized algorithms.

faculty.name: Ian Tice
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on partial differential equations and fluid mechanics, including free boundary problems for viscous fluids.

faculty.name: Noel J. Walkington
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in numerical analysis and scientific computing, including finite element methods for PDE and problems in continuum mechanics.

faculty.name: Theresa Anderson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Harmonic analysis and analytic number theory
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of harmonic analysis, number theory, and probability, including additive combinatorics and Diophantine problems.

faculty.name: Florian Frick
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in topological combinatorics and discrete geometry, applying topological methods to combinatorial problems.

faculty.name: Robin Neumayer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Geometric analysis and calculus of variations
faculty.summary: Works in geometric analysis and the calculus of variations, including isoperimetric and functional inequalities and their stability.

faculty.name: Agoston Pisztora
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory and statistical mechanics, including percolation and random media.

faculty.name: Konstantin Tikhomirov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability, random matrix theory, high-dimensional convex geometry, and combinatorics.

faculty.name: Tomasz Tkocz
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability and high-dimensional convex geometry, including geometry of random structures and functional inequalities.

faculty.name: Michael Young
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in combinatorics and graph theory, including Ramsey theory and rainbow colorings, and is active in efforts to broaden participation in mathematics.

faculty.name: Nicholas Boffi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in applied mathematics and machine learning, including generative modeling, high-dimensional dynamics, and control.

faculty.name: Christopher Eur
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in combinatorial algebraic geometry, including matroids, toric geometry, and Chow rings.

faculty.name: Matthew Rosenzweig
faculty.title: The Gregg Zeitlin Assistant Professor of Mathematical Sciences
faculty.summary: Works in analysis and mathematical physics, including mean-field limits, kinetic equations, and interacting particle systems.

faculty.name: Steve Awodey
faculty.title: Professor of Philosophy (courtesy appointment in Mathematical Sciences)
faculty.summary: Works in categorical logic and the foundations of mathematics, including homotopy type theory. Courtesy appointment in Mathematical Sciences.

faculty.name: Javier Peña
faculty.title: Professor of Operations Research (courtesy appointment in Mathematical Sciences)
faculty.summary: Works on the theory and algorithms of convex optimization and their numerical and computational aspects. Courtesy appointment in Mathematical Sciences.

requirements.admission_model: Direct admission to department; PhD-only (MA en route)

funding.note: Fully funded (GSAS fellowship: tuition, fees, stipend)

faculty.name: David Bayer
faculty.title: Professor (Barnard College)
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic geometry. Ph.D. Harvard 1982. On leave AY 2024-2025.

faculty.name: Andrew J. Blumberg
faculty.title: Herbert and Florence Irving Professor of Cancer Data Research, and Professor of Mathematics and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Research spans algebraic topology (equivariant stable homotopy theory, K-theory, cyclotomic spectra) and applications of topological data analysis to genomics and cancer biology. Joint appointment in Mathematics and Computer Science.

faculty.name: Simon Brendle
faculty.title: Adrain Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: World-leading differential geometer. Solved the Differentiable Sphere Theorem, Lawson Conjecture, and convergence of Yamabe flow. Received the 2024 Breakthrough Prize in Mathematics for transformative contributions to differential geometry.

faculty.name: Marco Castronovo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Barnard College)
faculty.summary: Works on symplectic geometry and topology, including Fukaya categories, Lagrangian cobordisms, cluster structures in positroid varieties, and Landau-Ginzburg models. Develops open-source computational tools for symplectic geometry.

faculty.name: Ivan Corwin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the interface of probability theory and mathematical physics, focusing on integrable probability, the KPZ universality class, interacting particle systems, and stochastic PDEs. Ph.D. Courant Institute, 2011.

faculty.name: Panagiota Daskalopoulos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in nonlinear partial differential equations and geometric analysis, including geometric flows, free boundary problems, and regularity theory. Ph.D. University of Chicago, 1992.

faculty.name: Johan de Jong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in algebraic geometry, with contributions to the theory of algebraic stacks, alterations, and the Stacks Project — an open-source comprehensive reference for algebraic stacks. Ph.D. Nijmegen, 1992.

faculty.name: Daniela De Silva
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair (Barnard College)
faculty.summary: Research in elliptic PDEs and harmonic analysis, with focus on free boundary problems, fully nonlinear elliptic equations, and nonlocal operators. Ph.D. MIT, 2005.

faculty.name: Julien Dubedat
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research in probability theory and mathematical physics, including Schramm-Loewner evolution (SLE), conformal field theory, and exactly solvable lattice models. Ph.D. Universite Paris-Sud, 2004.

faculty.name: Robert Friedman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic geometry, especially algebraic surfaces, vector bundles, Calabi-Yau manifolds, and degenerations of algebraic varieties. Ph.D. Harvard, 1981. Elected 2026 AMS Fellow.

faculty.name: Soren Galatius
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on algebraic topology, particularly moduli spaces of manifolds, homology of automorphism groups of free groups, cobordism categories, and tropical geometry. Clay Research Award (2022). Joined Columbia in 2025. Ph.D. Aarhus University, 2004.

faculty.name: Elena Giorgi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: General Relativity, Hyperbolic PDEs, Differential Geometry
faculty.summary: Research at the interface of general relativity, hyperbolic PDEs, and differential geometry, studying the stability of black hole solutions and gravitational waves. Ph.D. Columbia University, 2019.

faculty.name: Dorian Goldfeld
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in analytic number theory, automorphic forms, and L-functions. Known for the Goldfeld-Hoffstein-Lockhart theorem and fundamental work on the Gauss class number problem. Ph.D. Columbia, 1969.

faculty.name: Brian Greene
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in mathematical physics and string theory, including mirror symmetry, Calabi-Yau manifold topology change, and quantum gravity. Also known for extensive science communication and public outreach. Ph.D. Oxford, 1987.

faculty.name: Michael Harris
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Graduate Studies (PhD)
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in number theory and automorphic forms, with fundamental contributions to the Langlands program, Shimura varieties, and arithmetic geometry. Member of the American Philosophical Society. Ph.D. Harvard, 1977.

faculty.name: Ioannis Karatzas
faculty.title: Higgins Professor of Applied Probability
faculty.summary: Research in probability theory, stochastic processes, and their applications to mathematical finance and stochastic control. Co-author of the classic text 'Brownian Motion and Stochastic Calculus.' Ph.D. Columbia, 1980.

faculty.name: Alisa Knizel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Barnard College)
faculty.sub_field: Probability, Random Matrix Theory, Statistical Mechanics
faculty.summary: Research at the interface of probability, random matrix theory, and statistical mechanics, focusing on interacting particle systems, the KPZ universality class, and orthogonal polynomial ensembles. Ph.D. MIT, 2017.

faculty.name: Chao Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor (with tenure)
faculty.sub_field: Number Theory, Arithmetic Geometry, Automorphic Forms
faculty.summary: Research in number theory and arithmetic geometry, focusing on the arithmetic Siegel-Weil formula, Beilinson-Bloch conjecture for unitary Shimura varieties, and Kudla-Rapoport conjectures. David Goss Prize in Number Theory (2024). Ph.D. Harvard, 2015.

faculty.name: Francesco Lin
faculty.title: Associate Professor (with tenure)
faculty.summary: Research in low-dimensional topology and geometry, using gauge theory, Floer homology, and elliptic PDEs to study the structure of 3-manifolds and 4-manifolds. Ph.D. MIT, 2016.

faculty.name: Chiu-Chu (Melissa) Liu
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic geometry and symplectic geometry, with contributions to Gromov-Witten theory, mirror symmetry, and moduli spaces. Ph.D. Harvard, 2002.

faculty.name: Lucas Mason-Brown
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in representation theory, focusing on Lie algebras, algebraic groups, and their representations.

faculty.name: Dusa McDuff
faculty.title: Helen Lyttle Kimmel Professor (Barnard College)
faculty.summary: Pioneer of modern symplectic geometry. Co-author of the foundational texts on J-holomorphic curves and symplectic topology. Recent work on symplectic embeddings of ellipsoids and ellipsoidal superpotentials. Member of the National Academy of Sciences and Fellow of the Royal Society.

faculty.name: Marcel Nutz
faculty.title: Professor (Statistics, cross-listed in Mathematics)
faculty.summary: Research in probability theory, mathematical finance, and optimal transport. Work includes martingale optimal transport, mean field games, and robust finance. IMS Fellow (2023). IMS Medallion Award (2024). Ph.D. ETH Zurich, 2010.

faculty.name: Andrei Okounkov
faculty.title: Samuel Eilenberg Professor of Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Representation Theory, Algebraic Geometry, Mathematical Physics
faculty.summary: Fields Medalist (2006) for contributions bridging representation theory, algebraic geometry, and mathematical physics. Research includes enumerative geometry, quantum groups, and geometric representation theory. Ph.D. Moscow State University, 1995.

faculty.name: Lue Pan
faculty.title: Associate Professor (with tenure)
faculty.sub_field: Number Theory, Arithmetic Geometry, p-adic Galois Representations
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic number theory and the p-adic Langlands program, with work on the Fontaine-Mazur conjecture, completed cohomology of modular curves, and p-adic geometry of Shimura varieties. Published in Annals of Mathematics (2026).

faculty.name: Duong Hong Phong
faculty.title: Davies Professor of Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Complex Analysis and Geometry, PDEs, Mathematical Physics
faculty.summary: Research in complex analysis and geometry, partial differential equations, and mathematical physics, including contributions to string theory, geometric analysis, and several complex variables. Ph.D. Princeton, 1977.

faculty.name: Semon Rezchikov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Barnard College)
faculty.summary: Research in symplectic geometry and algebraic topology, including Floer homology, noncommutative differential forms, and Cartier formulae. Ph.D. Columbia University, 2021.

faculty.name: Giulia Sacca
faculty.title: Associate Professor (with tenure)
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic geometry, particularly hyper-Kahler manifolds, moduli spaces of sheaves on K3 surfaces, and derived categories. Ph.D. Princeton University, 2013.

faculty.name: Ovidiu Savin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in elliptic partial differential equations, with major contributions to free boundary problems, fully nonlinear elliptic equations, and regularity theory. Ph.D. University of Texas at Austin, 2003.

faculty.name: Mehtaab Sawhney
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the interface of combinatorics, probability, and analytic number theory, studying the interplay between structured and random/pseudorandom behavior. 2025 Packard Fellow. Clay Research Fellow. Ph.D. MIT, 2024.

faculty.name: Michael Thaddeus
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic geometry, including moduli spaces of vector bundles, geometric invariant theory, and connections with symplectic geometry and gauge theory. Ph.D. Oxford, 1992.

faculty.name: Eric Jean-Paul Urban
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in number theory, specializing in automorphic forms, Iwasawa theory, p-adic modular forms, and special values of L-functions. Ph.D. University of Paris Sud (Orsay), 1995.

faculty.name: Mu-Tao Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Differential Geometry, Geometric PDEs, General Relativity
faculty.summary: Research in differential geometry and geometric PDEs, with contributions to general relativity (quasi-local mass definitions), mean curvature flow, and minimal surfaces. Ph.D. Harvard, 1998.

faculty.name: Michael I. Weinstein
faculty.title: Professor (Mathematics and Applied Physics/Applied Mathematics)
faculty.sub_field: Applied Mathematics, Nonlinear PDEs, Waves in Nonlinear Media
faculty.summary: Research in applied mathematics focusing on nonlinear PDEs and analysis, waves in nonlinear, inhomogeneous, and random media, dynamical systems, and mathematical physics. Joint appointment in Mathematics and APAM. Ph.D. Courant Institute, 1982. Elected to American Academy of Arts and Sciences (2025).

requirements.admission_model: Departmental committee admission (SEAS); MS/PhD track available

funding.note: Comprehensive doctoral funding package: stipend, full tuition, health insurance and fees

faculty.name: Daniel Bienstock
faculty.title: Liu Family Professor of Industrial Engineering and Operations Research, and Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of optimization theory, methodology, and high-performance computation. Focuses on large-scale non-convex and discrete optimization with applications to power transmission networks, cascading failures, and grid security. 2022 INFORMS Khachiyan Prize recipient.

faculty.name: Liliana Borcea
faculty.title: George P. Livanos Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Wave Propagation, Inverse Problems, Scientific Computing
faculty.summary: Research in wave propagation in random media, inverse problems for PDEs, and data-driven reduced order modeling. Pioneered novel approaches to waveform inversion that mitigate cycle-skipping problems in full waveform inversion. Joined Columbia in July 2024. SIAM Fellow (2018), elected to American Academy of Arts and Sciences (2023).

faculty.name: Qiang Du
faculty.title: Fu Foundation Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Analysis, Scientific Computing, Nonlocal Models
faculty.summary: Research in mathematical modeling, numerical analysis, and scientific computation with applications in physical, biological, materials, and data sciences. Leads the Computational Mathematics and Multiscale Modeling (CM3) group. Known for work on nonlocal models and asymptotically compatible schemes. 2024 Frontiers of Science Award. Fellow of SIAM, AMS, and AAAS. Elected to American Academy of Arts and Sciences (2026).

faculty.name: Lorenzo M. Polvani
faculty.title: Maurice Ewing and J. Lamar Worzel Professor of Geophysics, and Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Atmospheric and Climate Dynamics, Geophysical Fluid Dynamics
faculty.summary: Research in atmospheric and climate dynamics, geophysical fluid dynamics, and the physical response of the climate system to increasing CO2. Studies stratospheric chemistry and dynamics, Arctic and Antarctic climate change, and Montreal Protocol impacts. Fellow of AAAS, AGU, and AMS.

faculty.name: Kui Ren
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Research in numerical analysis and scientific computing, with focus on inverse problems and imaging (diffuse optical tomography, photoacoustic tomography), kinetic modeling and simulation, random graphs and networks, and computational learning. 2026 SIAM Fellow. 2017 Calderon Prize. 2025 Feng Kang Prize.

faculty.name: Adam H. Sobel
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics, and of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Atmospheric and Climate Dynamics, Tropical Meteorology, Extreme Weather
faculty.summary: Research in atmospheric and climate dynamics with emphasis on the tropics, including tropical cyclones, intraseasonal variability, and severe convection. Directs Columbia's Initiative on Extreme Weather and Climate. 2026 AMS Joanne Simpson Tropical Meteorology Research Award. 2023 AGU Fellow.

faculty.name: Marc W. Spiegelman
faculty.title: Arthur D. Storke Memorial Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences, Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics, and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research in multi-physics computation of coupled fluid-solid dynamics, with emphasis on magma migration in the Earth's interior. Developed the TerraFERMA open-source finite element modeling framework for multi-physics problems. Also works on reactive cracking for carbon sequestration and earthquake physics. AGU Fellow (2022). Department Chair since 2021.

faculty.name: Michael K. Tippett
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Climate Predictability, Statistical Methods for Climate
faculty.summary: Research on the predictability and variability of the climate system, applying statistical and mathematical methods to observational and model data. First evidence for skillful prediction of monthly U.S. tornado activity. Co-author of 'Statistical Methods for Climate Scientists' (Cambridge, 2022). Chief Data Scientist, Initiative on Extreme Weather and Climate. Ph.D. Courant Institute, 1992.

faculty.name: Chris H. Wiggins
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Mathematical Biology, Machine Learning, Network Inference
faculty.summary: Applied mathematician whose research reframes biological network inference and analysis as machine learning problems. Works on transcriptional regulatory networks, biopolymer dynamics, and comparative genomics. Also Chief Data Scientist at The New York Times. APS Fellow (2014). Co-author of 'How Data Happened.' Ph.D. Princeton, 1998.

faculty.name: Michael I. Weinstein
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics, and Professor of Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Applied Mathematics, Nonlinear PDEs, Waves in Nonlinear Media
faculty.summary: Research in applied and fundamental mathematics focusing on nonlinear PDEs and analysis, waves in nonlinear, inhomogeneous, and random media, dynamical systems, and mathematical physics with applications to optics and photonics. Joint appointment between APAM and Mathematics. Ph.D. Courant Institute, 1982. Elected to American Academy of Arts and Sciences (2025).

faculty.name: Kyle T. Mandli
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Methods for PDEs, Coastal Hazards, Scientific Computing
faculty.summary: Research in computational and analytical aspects of geophysical shallow mass flows, including storm surge, tsunami, and coastal flooding. Develops numerical methods for hyperbolic PDEs, adaptive mesh refinement, and uncertainty quantification. Ph.D. University of Washington, 2011.

faculty.name: Curtiss Lyman
faculty.title: Ju Tang Chu and Wui Ping Chu Assistant Professor of Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Research on partial differential equations arising from condensed matter physics and quantum materials, applying Floquet-Bloch theory, spectral theory, representation theory, and complex analysis. Joined Columbia in Fall 2025. Ph.D. University of Washington, 2025.

faculty.name: Qihao Ye
faculty.title: Limited-Term Assistant Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Analysis, Scientific Computing, Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of numerical analysis, scientific computing, and machine learning, with emphasis on mathematically grounded computational methods for PDEs, nonlocal models, stochastic dynamics, and data-driven inference. Also works on AI-assisted mathematical research and formal reasoning. Joining July 2026. Ph.D. UC San Diego, 2026.

requirements.admission_model: Departmental committee admission (SEAS); MS/PhD track available

funding.note: Comprehensive doctoral funding package: stipend, full tuition, health insurance and fees

faculty.name: Michal Lipson
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow and one of the world's leading researchers in silicon photonics. Pioneered the development of silicon-based photonic devices for optical interconnects, sensing, and quantum information. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Allen H. Boozer
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Plasma theorist working on the theory of magnetic confinement for fusion energy, magnetic field line Hamiltonian dynamics, and nonlinear dynamics. Foundational contributions to stellarator optimization theory.

faculty.name: Alexander L. Gaeta
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Electrical Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Optical and Laser Physics / Nonlinear Optics
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in ultrafast and nonlinear optics and nanophotonics, including nonlinear propagation in fibers and bulk media, on-chip frequency combs, and coherent interactions of laser light with matter. Recipient of the 2026 Arthur L. Schawlow Prize in Laser Science.

faculty.name: Latha Venkataraman
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Pioneer of single-molecule electronics; developed the STM break-junction technique to measure electron transport and mechanics through individual molecules, establishing fundamental understanding of charge transport at the single-molecule scale.

faculty.name: Michael I. Weinstein
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Distinguished applied mathematician known for rigorous analysis of nonlinear waves in periodic and inhomogeneous media. Contributions to understanding of defect modes in photonic crystals, soliton dynamics, and topological edge states. Fellow of the AMS and SIAM.

faculty.name: Lorenzo M. Polvani
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics and of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Leading climate scientist studying atmospheric dynamics and climate change. Research on the atmospheric response to ozone depletion and greenhouse gases, stratosphere-troposphere coupling, and Arctic amplification. Fellow of the American Geophysical Union.

faculty.name: Qiang Du
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Leading applied mathematician working on numerical methods for partial differential equations, nonlocal and fractional models, and phase field approaches. Major contributions to finite element methods and scientific computing. Fellow of SIAM, AMS, and AAAS.

faculty.name: Oleg Gang
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Chemical Engineering
faculty.summary: Leader in DNA-directed self-assembly of nanoscale systems. Uses DNA as a programmable linker to create complex 3D nanoparticle architectures with applications in photonics, catalysis, and drug delivery. Director of the Center for Programmable Assembly at Columbia.

faculty.name: Liliana Borcea
faculty.title: George P. Livanos Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Expert in wave propagation in random and complex media. Develops mathematical theory and computational methods for inverse scattering and imaging, with applications to seismic exploration, medical imaging, and radar. Fellow of SIAM.

faculty.name: Renata Wentzcovitch
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Leading computational physicist modeling materials at extreme conditions. Pioneered computational mineral physics, providing first-principles predictions of mineral properties in Earth's deep interior. Fellow of the American Geophysical Union and Mineralogical Society of America.

faculty.name: Gerald A. Navratil
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental plasma physicist focused on plasma diagnostics, magnetohydrodynamic stability, and control in tokamaks for fusion energy science.

faculty.name: Adam H. Sobel
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics and of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Leading atmospheric scientist studying tropical meteorology and climate. Research on the dynamics of tropical cyclones, monsoons, and extreme weather events in a changing climate. Frequent public communicator on climate science.

faculty.name: Nanfang Yu
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Nanophotonics researcher known for metasurface-based flat optics and mid- and far-infrared optical and optoelectronic devices, active plasmonics, and metamaterials, including bio-inspired photonic systems.

faculty.name: Carlos Paz-Soldan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental plasma physicist studying magnetically confined plasmas, plasma stability and control, runaway electrons, and disruption mitigation, and fusion energy technologies. APS Fellow (2024).

faculty.name: Chris A. Marianetti
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Computational physicist predicting materials properties from first-principles computations, combining density-functional theory and dynamical mean-field theory to study transition-metal oxides, actinides, and energy-related materials.

faculty.name: Simon J. L. Billinge
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Materials Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Pioneer of the atomic pair distribution function (PDF) method for determining the local structure of nanostructured and disordered materials. His techniques are widely used at synchrotron and neutron facilities worldwide.

faculty.name: Marc W. Spiegelman
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Current Department Chair. Expert in computational geophysics focusing on magma migration, fluid-solid interaction in Earth's interior, and multi-phase flow. Develops advanced numerical methods for geophysical fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Kui Ren
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Applied mathematician developing theory and computational methods for inverse problems and uncertainty quantification. Applications span medical imaging, geophysics, and material characterization. Major awards for contributions to computational mathematics.

faculty.name: Daniel Bienstock
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics and of Industrial Engineering and Operations Research
faculty.summary: Leading optimization researcher working on algorithms for preventing national-scale blackouts in power grids. His work on robust optimization and network flows has had significant practical impact on critical infrastructure security.

faculty.name: Chris H. Wiggins
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Data scientist and applied mathematician working at the interface of machine learning and biology. Research spans Bayesian inference, network analysis in computational biology, and data ethics. Former Chief Data Scientist of The New York Times.

faculty.name: Irving P. Herman
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist studying optical properties of nanocrystals and nanostructured materials. Research includes nanoparticle assembly, laser diagnostics for thin films, and nanomaterial photophysics. Author of widely used textbook on optical diagnostics.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Paul
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Plasma theorist developing methods for the theory of magnetic confinement of plasmas, stellarator optimization, and PDE-constrained/shape optimization for fusion energy design. DOE Early Career Award recipient.

faculty.name: Xueyue (Sherry) Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist developing quantum devices, including superconducting qubits, solid-state spins, quantum light-matter interaction, topological photonics, and quantum networking and information.

faculty.name: Yuan Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics and of Materials Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Materials scientist developing nanostructured materials for electrochemical energy storage and thermal management. Research on next-generation batteries, electrochromic devices, and thermal interface materials. Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Michele Simoncelli
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Early-career theorist developing quantum mechanical theories of charge and heat transport in materials. Known for the Wigner formulation of transport with wide applications from thermoelectrics to neuromorphic computing materials.

faculty.name: Ben Zhu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical and computational plasma physicist studying the nonlinear dynamics of magnetized plasmas, boundary plasma turbulence and transport, and disruptions in tokamaks and stellarators using high-performance computing. Joined Columbia in 2026.

faculty.name: William E. Bailey
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Materials Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed-matter physicist studying nanoscale magnetic films and heterostructures, materials issues in spin-polarized transport, and materials engineering of magnetization dynamics.

faculty.name: Aravind Devarakonda
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist working on quantum materials synthesis, nanodevice fabrication, and low-temperature physics, including superconductivity, strongly-correlated matter, and topologically ordered phases.

faculty.name: Steven A. Sabbagh
faculty.title: Adjunct Professor and Senior Research Scientist of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Senior research scientist and principal investigator leading a plasma physics research group on macroscopic (MHD) stability of magnetically confined fusion plasmas and disruption prediction and avoidance on tokamaks (NSTX-U, KSTAR); directs a Columbia-PPPL student research collaboration.

funding.note: Comprehensive doctoral funding package: stipend, full tuition, health insurance and fees
Fully funded (tuition, stipend, health insurance) for PhD students

faculty.name: Marcelo Aguiar
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic combinatorics, including combinatorial Hopf algebras, species, and category theory. Serves as Director of Graduate Studies.

faculty.name: Dan M. Barbasch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in the representation theory of reductive Lie groups, particularly the classification of unitary representations and harmonic analysis.

faculty.name: Yuri Berest
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies

faculty.name: Garth Isaak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on graph theory and combinatorics, including generalizations of interval graphs and orders, sum list coloring, star critical Ramsey numbers, and generalizations of DeBruijn cycles. faculty.title: Professor

Has published over 70 papers.

faculty.title: Assistant Professor

faculty.name: Xianyi Zeng
faculty.title: Assistant Professor

faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Research focuses on probability theory and stochastic processes with applications.

faculty.name: Andrew Harder
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of algebraic geometry and mathematical physics, using Hodge-theoretic tools for problems from physics. Research includes mirror symmetry, Calabi-Yau varieties, Landau-Ginzburg models, holomorphic symplectic geometry, and connections between Feynman integrals and period functions of algebraic varieties.

faculty.name: Si Tang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on probability theory and stochastic processes with applications.

faculty.name: Ping-Shi Wu
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Chair

faculty.name: Lei Wu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on differential equations arising from mathematical physics, including kinetic theory, fluid mechanics, solid mechanics, quantum mechanics, and relativity. Studies existence, uniqueness, continuous dependence on data, long-term stability, bifurcation, and asymptotic limits.

faculty.name: Taeho Kim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Balances mathematical and applied statistics. Developed multiple interval estimation procedures applied to leukemia gene expression data and created prediction intervals for U.S. COVID-19 deaths using Poisson regression. Latest work involves new prediction approaches maximizing concordance correlation as an alternative to least-squares methods.

faculty.name: Zi Ye
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in multivariate and nonparametric analysis with novel statistical methodology and companion theory. Addresses misclassification errors in diagnostic devices for real-world clinical trials, bridging the gap in evaluating treatment validity when diagnostic errors exist. Published in Pharmaceutical Statistics on multivariate treatment effects in contaminated clinical trials.

faculty.name: Xianyi Zeng
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops numerical methods for PDEs in emerging fields, especially hyperbolic conservation laws with moving or complex geometries. Projects include superconvergent hybrid-variable discretization, shifted boundary methods for compressible Euler equations, embedded boundary multi-material flow computation on moving grids, cellular dynamics modeling of virus infection and virotherapy in tumors, and meandering instability in fluid-like systems. Ph.D. in Computational Mathematics from Stanford (2012)

faculty.name: Ao Sun
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans geometry, topology, and differential equations. Ph.D. from MIT (2020). Work includes geometric PDEs with potential applications in physics and engineering.

faculty.name: Terry Napier
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the Levi problem in complex geometry, determining conditions on topology, geometry, and potential theory of Kähler complex manifolds that imply existence of many holomorphic functions, with connections to complex analysis and geometric PDEs.

programs.program_name: Engineering Sciences and Applied Mathematics PhD

funding.note: Nearly all admitted receive support; fellowship year 1, then RA/TA (min $3,080/mo, tuition, health)

faculty.name: Daniel Abrams
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Abrams develops mathematical and dynamical-systems models to understand complex phenomena ranging from synchronization and chimera states in oscillator networks to social dynamics such as opinion polarization and language competition. He is Co-Director of the Northwestern Institute on Complex Systems (NICO).

faculty.name: Luis A. Nunes Amaral
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Amaral's work applies quantitative and network-based approaches to diverse challenges, from forecasting transportation network evolution to analyzing clinical states from electronic health records and studying citation patterns in science. He has published over 180 peer-reviewed papers in journals such as Nature, Science, PNAS, and Cell.

faculty.name: Alvin Bayliss
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bayliss develops and analyzes computational models of multi-species ecological communities, with a focus on cyclic competition dynamics and pattern formation. He serves as Director of Graduate Studies for ESAM.

faculty.name: David Chopp
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chopp develops numerical algorithms and computational models for interface-driven phenomena. His work spans biofilm growth and signaling, phase field modeling of materials, and fracture mechanics using level set methods.

faculty.name: William Kath
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kath's work centers on mathematical modeling of biological systems, particularly circadian rhythms and neural signaling. He has contributed to understanding how temperature drives circadian gene regulation and how sensory systems enable rapid threat assessment.

faculty.name: Diego Klabjan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Klabjan's work focuses on machine learning and deep learning with applications in finance, transportation, sports, and bioinformatics. He is Director of the Center for Deep Learning and the MS in Machine Learning and Data Science program.

faculty.name: Erik Luijten
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Computational Materials Science and Soft Matter
faculty.summary: Luijten's group uses computer simulations to understand experimental phenomena from microscopic system features and to design new materials. His projects span self-assembly, charge transport in electrolytes, programmable/active matter, and dielectric materials.

faculty.name: Michael J. Miksis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Miksis's work centers on fluid dynamics at small scales and interfaces, spanning electrohydrodynamic flows around colloidal particles, vesicle and droplet shape dynamics, and capillary phenomena. He serves as Director of MS Studies for ESAM.

faculty.name: Adilson Motter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Motter's group investigates cascading dynamics, spontaneous synchronization, network control, and symmetry phenomena with applications to quantum networks, machine learning, power grids, microfluidics, and metamaterials. He directs the Center for Network Dynamics.

faculty.name: Jorge Nocedal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nocedal's work spans algorithm design, software implementation, and mathematical analysis of optimization methods. His research focuses on noise-tolerant optimization, adaptive sampling, and quasi-Newton algorithms with applications in machine learning and engineering design.

faculty.name: Neelesh A. Patankar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Patankar's lab develops computational fluid dynamics techniques for fluid-structure interaction problems and applies them to understand organ physiology in clinical contexts. The group also works on phase control using engineered surface textures for applications like anti-icing and condensation.

faculty.name: Hermann Riecke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Riecke's work centers on how plasticity restructures neuronal networks and how feedback from higher brain areas shapes information processing, with a particular focus on the olfactory system. He also studies pattern formation in spatially extended dynamical systems.

faculty.name: Petia Vlahovska
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vlahovska leads the Complex Fluids and Soft Interfaces Lab, integrating experimental and theoretical modeling of physical and biological systems. Her group explores biomembrane electromechanics, self-organization in active matter, and interfacial instabilities.

faculty.name: Vladimir Volpert
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Volpert investigates pattern formation and traveling waves in nonlinear systems, with applications ranging from combustion and frontal polymerization to cyclic ecological population dynamics. His recent work focuses on mathematical models of multi-species ecological systems.

faculty.name: Rosemary Braun
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Braun develops computational methods that combine bioinformatic information with experimental data to characterize molecular interaction networks. Her work aims to understand how these networks drive complex phenotypes, particularly cancers.

faculty.name: James Fitzgerald
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Fitzgerald's lab combines first-principles theory, phenomenological modeling, data analysis, and experimental design to build theoretical frameworks linking brain circuit activity to behavior. They seek general principles spanning across different systems and model organisms.

faculty.name: Madhav Mani
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Mani's group develops mathematical frameworks to illuminate biological processes, pairing data-driven methods with the aesthetics and sensibilities of mathematics and theoretical physics. They collaborate with experimental biology groups across organismal development, cellular physiology, and ecological dynamics.

faculty.name: Alexander (Sasha) Tchekhovskoy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Tchekhovskoy studies how black holes and neutron stars consume stars, launch relativistic jets, and influence galaxy evolution. His work involves large-scale numerical simulations to model accretion disks and jets, linking simulations to observational data.

faculty.name: Istvan Kovacs
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Kovacs develops novel methodologies to predict emerging structural and functional patterns across fields from systems biology to quantum physics, working closely with experimental groups. His research bridges structure and function in complex systems.

faculty.name: Daniel Lecoanet
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lecoanet studies fluid dynamics in astrophysical and geophysical contexts, such as stellar interiors and planetary atmospheres, using theoretical approaches and numerical simulations. He is a core developer of the Dedalus spectral-method PDE solver.

faculty.name: Yiping Lu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lu studies when, why, and how machine-learning systems improve predictably as resources such as data, model size, and compute are scaled. His work aims to build a general theory of scalable learning in which increased resources provably and reliably lead to better performance.

faculty.name: Niall Mangan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Mangan works at the intersection of mechanistic modeling and data science, connecting the speed and automation of data-driven model selection with the explanatory power of bottom-up mechanistic modeling. Her work spans biological network inference, microbial engineering, and solar cell optimization.

faculty.name: Krishna Shrinivas
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: The Shrinivas lab builds computational frameworks to predict and engineer the processes that power life at the molecular and cellular scales, blending biology, physics, machine learning, and engineering. Current work spans genome organization, physics-driven AI for biomolecule design, and computation in biological systems.

faculty.name: Lydia Kavraki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kenneth and Audrey Kennedy Professor working at the intersection of computational science, robotics, and biology. Research applies computational methods to biomolecular interactions, protein function prediction, and drug design.

faculty.name: John M. Mellor-Crummey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and leader in high-performance computing. Creator of HPCToolkit for performance analysis of parallel scientific applications. Research focuses on tools and compilers for scalable scientific computing on supercomputers.

faculty.name: Keith D. Cooper
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and co-author of 'Engineering a Compiler.' Research focuses on compiler construction and code optimization for high-performance scientific and engineering computing applications.

faculty.name: Vladimir Braverman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Victor E. Cameron Chair Professor working on algorithms for computational science. Research designs efficient methods for processing massive scientific datasets, with applications in genomics and radiomics.

faculty.name: Luay Nakhleh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and Dean of Engineering. Research develops computational methods and algorithms for evolutionary biology, modeling biological networks and phylogenomic relationships from genomic data.

faculty.name: Todd Treangen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor developing computational methods for genomics. Research focuses on algorithms for analyzing microbial genomes, metagenomic data, and tracking pathogen evolution and outbreaks.

faculty.name: Tirthak Patel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Quantum Computing Systems, HPC, Scientific Computing
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on quantum computing systems and HPC. Research develops system-level solutions for practical quantum computing, including error mitigation and hybrid quantum-classical workflows for scientific computing.

faculty.name: Moshe Vardi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Formal Methods, Automated Reasoning, Computational Logic
faculty.summary: University Professor and leading expert in formal methods and computational logic. Research applies automated reasoning to verification of hardware and software systems through foundational algorithmic techniques.

faculty.name: Konstantinos Mamouras
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Formal Methods, Stream Processing, Automata Theory
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on formal methods and stream processing. Research develops formal foundations for specifying and verifying data stream computations, with applications in real-time analytics and IoT systems.

faculty.name: Arlei Silva
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Machine Learning, Graph Mining, Computational Science
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on ML and graph mining with applications to computational science, including climate forecasting, disaster response, and urban resilience modeling.

faculty.name: Vicky Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on computational biology and bioinformatics. Research develops algorithms and models for analyzing high-throughput biological data to understand disease mechanisms at molecular and systems levels.

faculty.name: Nai-Hui Chia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on quantum computing theory. Research focuses on designing quantum algorithms and understanding the fundamental computational power and limitations of quantum systems.

faculty.name: Alan Reid
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Edgar Odell Lovett Professor of Mathematics and Department Chair. Reid studies hyperbolic manifolds, low-dimensional topology, and geometric group theory, with a particular interest in connections between hyperbolic geometry, number theory, and moduli spaces.

faculty.name: David Damanik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Robert L. Moody, Sr. Professor of Mathematics. Damanik works on spectral theory and dynamical systems, with emphasis on the mathematics of quasicrystals and aperiodic order. His research explores Schrödinger operators with ergodic or quasi-periodic potentials, analyzing Lyapunov exponents, Cantor spectrum, quantum transport, and localization phenomena.

faculty.name: David Fisher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Milton B. Porter Professor of Mathematics. Fisher works on rigidity in dynamics and geometry, with a focus on Lie groups, discrete groups, and ergodic theory. His research emphasizes dynamics of large group actions and ergodic geometry.

faculty.name: Shelly Harvey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Mathematics. Harvey works in low-dimensional topology and geometry, studying knots, links, and 3- and 4-dimensional manifolds. Her research examines the classification of knots through concordance, filtrations of knot groups, and higher-order signature invariants.

faculty.name: Chris Leininger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Mathematics and Director of Graduate Studies. Leininger works at the intersection of geometry, topology, group theory, and dynamics in low dimensions, with contributions to Teichmüller theory, mapping class groups, and billiards. Fellow of the American Mathematical Society (2023).

faculty.name: Stephen Semmes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Noah Harding Professor of Mathematics. Semmes' research spans harmonic analysis, complex variables, partial differential equations, and differential geometry, focusing on the analysis of functions and equations arising in mathematical physics and geometry.

faculty.name: Anthony Varilly-Alvarado
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Mathematics. Varilly-Alvarado's research centers on arithmetic geometry, particularly rational points on algebraic varieties, Brauer-Manin obstructions, and the arithmetic of del Pezzo and K3 surfaces. He also applies algebraic geometry to coding theory via algebraic curves and surfaces.

faculty.name: Chelsea Walton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Mathematics. Walton specializes in noncommutative and quantum algebra, focusing on quantum symmetries of algebras and algebraic structures in categories. She is authoring the three-volume book series 'Symmetries of Algebras: Algebraic Quantum Symmetry.'

faculty.name: Christos Mantoulidis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Mathematics. Mantoulidis works on differential geometry, partial differential equations, mathematical general relativity, geometric measure theory, and geometric flows. Recipient of the Frontiers of Science Award (2024) and Rice Rising Star Award (2024).

faculty.name: Brandon Levin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Mathematics. Levin works in number theory and p-adic geometry, studying p-adic Hodge theory, Galois representations, and the Langlands program.

faculty.name: Zhiyong Gao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Mathematics. Gao works in differential geometry, applying methods from calculus and linear algebra to study the properties of curves, surfaces, and higher-dimensional manifolds. Ph.D. from SUNY Stony Brook (1984).

faculty.name: Gregory Chambers
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Mathematics. Chambers works in metric geometry and geometric analysis, focusing on quantitative topology, min-max theory, isoperimetric inequalities, and stability estimates for geometric inequalities. His research examines minimal hypersurfaces, geodesic nets, and optimal sweepouts of Riemannian surfaces.

faculty.name: Jo Nelson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Mathematics. Nelson works at the intersection of symplectic and contact topology, focusing on defining and computing contact homology invariants using pseudoholomorphic curve techniques. Her research has applications to Reeb dynamics, low-dimensional topology, and symplectic embedding problems.
