faculty.name: Kasso Okoudjou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research covers pure, applied, and numerical harmonic analysis, with focus on time-frequency analysis, wavelet analysis, frame theory, and analysis and differential equations on fractals and graphs. Also works on data science applications and mathematics education, particularly active learning and increasing underrepresented minority participation in undergraduate research.

faculty.name: James Adler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops efficient computational methods for complex fluids, plasma physics, electromagnetism, and other physical applications. Focuses on adaptive finite-element discretizations and multigrid solvers for nonlinear PDEs that model multi-scale physical systems, aiming to produce accurate, physics-preserving solutions with optimal computational efficiency.

faculty.name: Bruce Boghosian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Applied Dynamical Systems and Applied Probability
faculty.summary: Research centers on applied dynamical systems, applied probability theory, kinetic theory, and agent-based modeling. Currently emphasizes mathematical models of economics and agent-based models of wealth distributions, exploring wealth concentration, mobility, and oligarchy. Secondary work involves lattice Boltzmann models, kinetic theory, and quantum computation. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: Corey Bregman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Geometric Group Theory and Low-Dimensional Topology
faculty.summary: Works on geometric group theory, low-dimensional topology, and CAT(0) spaces. Earned PhD from Rice University in 2017. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: Fulton Gonzalez
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Noncommutative Harmonic Analysis and Integral Geometry
faculty.summary: Focuses on noncommutative harmonic analysis, representations of Lie groups, integral geometry, and Radon transforms. Studies reconstructing functions from indirect data such as averages over curves or surfaces, with applications in medical imaging and sonar.

faculty.name: Boris Hasselblatt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on geometrically motivated hyperbolic dynamics within dynamical systems theory. Studies hyperbolic phenomena and geometrically motivated systems. Founding editor of the Journal of Modern Dynamics and founding Editor-in-Chief of Mathematics Research Reports.

faculty.name: Xiaozhe Hu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the development, analysis, and implementation of numerical methods for solving partial differential equations (PDEs) and graph problems. Specializes in parallel multigrid and multilevel methods for large-scale coupled systems, with applications in multiphase flow in porous media, complex fluids, magnetohydrodynamics, and fluid-structure interaction. Also works on GPU and multicore CPU parallelization of solvers.

faculty.name: Misha Kilmer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Linear Algebra and Scientific Computing
faculty.summary: Research focuses on numerical linear and multilinear algebra, scientific computing, and image reconstruction and restoration. SIAM Fellow (2021). Served as Deputy Director of ICERM at Brown University (2021-2025). Holds joint appointment in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Robert Lemke-Oliver
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on analytic number theory, with a particular focus on equidistribution problems and elliptic curves. Studies L-functions, modular forms, the distribution of prime numbers, and rational solutions to elliptic curve equations. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: George McNinch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and representations of algebraic groups, with a particular focus on linear algebraic groups in the modular setting where the coefficient field has prime characteristic. Research areas include linear representations, complete reducibility, conjugacy classes, Levi decompositions, and related cohomological issues.

faculty.name: James Murphy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans pure math, computational math, and applications to remote sensing, image processing, and medical data analysis. Theoretical focus includes harmonic analysis, machine learning, and theoretical statistics, with attention to unsupervised learning, spectral graph methods, and eigenfunctions of operators on graphs. Develops efficient, scalable algorithms for unsupervised learning. Applied work includes hyperspectral imagery analysis and ECG signal analysis. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Newman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Linear Algebra and Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Research focuses on multidimensional (multilinear) algebra, numerical linear algebra, machine learning, and deep neural networks. Works on tensor-based methods for data analysis including tensor neural networks, tensor-tensor products, and tensor decompositions for image compression, deblurring, and classification. Recipient of Air Force FY25 Young Investigator Program Award and 2025 SIAM CSE Best Paper Prize. 2025-2026 Merrin Faculty Fellow.

faculty.name: Samantha Petti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans computational biology, algorithms, probability, fitness landscapes, evolution, protein structure, and statistical inference. Holds joint appointment in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Kim Ruane
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geometric group theory, focusing on a class of groups similar to Fuchsian groups but much more general. Her work connects geometry, group theory, and topology to distinguish geometric objects by their symmetry groups. Began work on knot theory applications to DNA. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: David Smyth
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on algebraic geometry, focusing on the application of geometric methods to understand solutions of systems of polynomial equations. Particularly interested in curves, singularities, and moduli theory, aiming to establish systematic constructions and classifications of geometric compactifications of the moduli space of algebraic curves.

faculty.name: Abiy Tasissa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops scalable and robust algorithms at the intersection of applied mathematics, machine learning, and data science. Key areas include high-dimensional data analysis (matrix completion, compressive sensing), mathematical foundations of AI (interpretable machine learning, dictionary learning), and applied optimization (distance geometry, large-scale kernel methods, algorithm unrolling).

faculty.name: Montserrat Teixidor I Bigas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on algebraic curves and moduli spaces. Studies vector bundles on curves, which appear in physics through Gromov-Witten invariants and have provided beautiful proofs to long-standing conjectures as well as striking counterexamples.

faculty.name: Genevieve Walsh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Geometric topologist who studies groups acting on spaces, hyperbolic manifolds and orbifolds, low-dimensional topology, and group actions. Her work is deeply influenced by 3-dimensional hyperbolic manifolds and orbifolds, and has addressed topics including hyperbolic 2-bridge knot complements, the virtually Haken conjecture, commensurability of knot complements, and right-angled Coxeter groups.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Mina Aganagic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Aganagic's work sits at the intersection of mathematics and theoretical physics, using techniques from string theory and mirror symmetry to address problems in geometry and topology. Her research explores knot categorization, quantum Langlands correspondences, and topological vertex theory.

faculty.name: Suncica Canic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Canic's research focuses on mathematical modeling and analysis of partial differential equations arising in fluid dynamics, particularly fluid-structure interaction problems with applications to cardiovascular mechanics, hemodynamics, and medical device design.

faculty.name: Jennifer Chayes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chayes's research applies tools from probability and graph theory to problems in machine learning and complex networks, with a focus on mathematical structures like graphons for modeling large-scale systems. She holds joint appointments in EECS, Information, and Statistics.

faculty.name: Steven N. Evans
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Evans' research spans stochastic processes and their applications to mathematical biology, including phylogenetics, population genetics, and evolutionary dynamics. His work develops probabilistic models for biological systems with rigorous mathematical foundations.

faculty.name: Ming Gu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gu develops fast and stable algorithms for large-scale linear algebra problems, including matrix factorizations, eigenvalue computations, and structured linear systems, spanning both theoretical foundations and practical applications in scientific computing and data analysis.

faculty.name: Venkatesan Guruswami
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Guruswami's research lies at the intersection of computer science and mathematics, focusing on error-correcting codes, algebraic methods for computation, and approximate optimization. He also serves as Director of the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing.

faculty.name: Olga Holtz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Holtz's work lies at the intersection of numerical linear algebra, matrix theory, and combinatorics, focusing on communication costs and computational complexity of algorithms. She investigates structured matrices, root localization of polynomials, and communication-efficient matrix multiplication.

faculty.name: Michael J. Lindsey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lindsey's research develops numerical methods and mathematical theory for computational physics problems, with emphasis on electronic structure calculations, quantum many-body systems, and tensor network methods bridging applied mathematics and computational quantum science.

faculty.name: Lin Lin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lin develops mathematical and computational methods for quantum mechanical systems, spanning numerical algorithms for quantum chemistry (Kohn-Sham DFT) and quantum computing algorithms including Hamiltonian simulation, phase estimation, and ground-state preparation.

faculty.name: Per-Olof Persson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Persson's research focuses on computational mathematics and scientific computing, particularly unstructured mesh generation, high-order discontinuous Galerkin methods, and their applications to computational fluid dynamics, fluid-structure interaction, and wave propagation problems.

faculty.name: Fraydoun Rezakhanlou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rezakhanlou's work sits at the intersection of probability theory and PDEs, focusing on stochastic models and conservation laws. His research spans scalar conservation laws with random initial conditions, stochastic Navier-Stokes, particle systems, and multi-agent reinforcement learning.

faculty.name: James A. Sethian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sethian pioneered level set and fast marching methods for solving PDEs on irregular domains with moving interfaces, with applications spanning semiconductor manufacturing, seismic imaging, fluid dynamics, and tumor growth simulation.

faculty.name: Chris Shannon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Shannon applies rigorous mathematical tools including transversality theory, Lipschitz functions, and equilibrium analysis to foundational questions in economic theory, examining subjective beliefs, market stability, preference evolution, and strategic behavior.

faculty.name: Nikhil Srivastava
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Srivastava's work lies at the intersection of mathematics and theoretical computer science, focusing on random matrix theory and the geometry of polynomials. He co-resolved the Kadison-Singer problem and developed the theory of interlacing families, constructing bipartite Ramanujan graphs of all degrees.

faculty.name: Franziska Weber
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Weber's research centers on the theoretical and computational study of nonlinear partial differential equations, combining techniques from numerical analysis and applied mathematics. She is also affiliated with Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

faculty.name: Jon Wilkening
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wilkening develops numerical methods and computational algorithms to solve PDEs, with applications ranging from fluid dynamics (water waves, swimming sheets) to materials science and micromagnetics, combining mathematical analysis with high-performance computing.

faculty.name: Mariusz Wodzicki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wodzicki's research spans noncommutative geometry, cyclic homology, and algebraic K-theory, developing algebraic tools that bridge pure and applied mathematics, including work on determinants in infinite dimensions and zeta-regularized products.

faculty.name: Maciej Zworski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Zworski's research centers on semiclassical analysis and its applications to mathematical physics, particularly scattering theory and quantum chaos. He investigates resonances, quantum decay rates, and spectral properties of operators using microlocal analysis techniques.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Ian Agol
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor specializing in geometry and topology, particularly low-dimensional and hyperbolic geometry.

faculty.name: Richard Bamler
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate professor working in geometry/topology and geometric analysis, including Ricci flow.

faculty.name: Richard E. Borcherds
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in algebra and a Fields Medalist, known for vertex algebras and automorphic forms.

faculty.name: Ben Davison
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Davison's research bridges enumerative geometry and geometric representation theory, applying geometric counting problems to advance understanding of algebraic structures such as Lie algebras and their representations. He works on cohomological Donaldson-Thomas theory and sheaf counting on Calabi-Yau threefolds.

faculty.name: David Eisenbud
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in algebra, specializing in commutative algebra and algebraic geometry.

faculty.name: Edward Frenkel

faculty.name: Alexander Givental

faculty.title: Professor

funding.note: Guaranteed TA with full tuition & fee remission for 5 yrs

faculty.name: James McKernan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor (Charles Lee Powell Chair) in algebraic geometry, known for work on the minimal model program and birational geometry.

faculty.name: Kiran Kedlaya
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor (Stefan E. Warschawski Chair) working in number theory, arithmetic geometry, and p-adic methods.

faculty.name: Cristian Popescu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in number theory, working on Iwasawa theory and special values of L-functions.

faculty.name: Daniel Kane
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor (joint with Computer Science) working in theoretical computer science, learning theory, number theory, and combinatorics.

faculty.name: Peter Ebenfelt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ebenfelt's research spans complex variables, partial differential equations, and complex geometry. He earned his Ph.D. from the Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm and has served as department chair.

faculty.name: Bennett Chow
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in differential geometry known for work on the Ricci flow and geometric evolution equations.

faculty.name: Lei Ni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ni's research spans geometric analysis, Riemannian geometry, complex geometry and analysis, and partial differential equations. He is a Fellow of the AMS and a Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Luca Spolaor
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor working in geometric measure theory, calculus of variations, and PDE.

faculty.name: Andrej Zlatos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in partial differential equations, including fluid dynamics and reaction-diffusion equations.

faculty.name: Jacob Sterbenz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sterbenz studies non-linear geometric wave equations and dispersive PDE, with a primary focus on field equations in special and general relativity. His work also involves microlocal analysis and nonlinear wave equations.

faculty.name: Todd Kemp
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor (Vice Chair) working in probability, free probability, and random matrix theory.

faculty.name: Jason Schweinsberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in probability, working on coalescent processes and mathematical population genetics.

faculty.name: Tianyi Zheng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor working on random walks on groups, geometric group theory, and probability.

faculty.name: Dimitris Politis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor (Halicioglu Data Science Institute Chair) in statistics, working on time series and resampling methods.

faculty.name: Jelena Bradic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bradic's research spans high-dimensional statistics, stochastic optimization, asymptotic theory, and robust statistics, with applications to functional genomics and biostatistics. She earned her Ph.D. from Princeton University and has received the Hellman Fellowship.

faculty.name: Ery Arias-Castro
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor working in statistics and machine learning, including high-dimensional inference and geometric data analysis.

faculty.name: Ronghui (Lily) Xu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Xu's research in statistics and biostatistics focuses on survival analysis, clinical trials, epidemiology, and genetic epidemiology. She develops statistical methodology for biomedical applications including cancer research and genomic studies.

faculty.name: Ioana Dumitriu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor working in random matrix theory, probability, and numerical linear algebra.

faculty.name: Rayan Saab
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in applied mathematics, working on compressed sensing, quantization, and signal processing.

faculty.name: Melvin Leok
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in computational mathematics, specializing in geometric numerical integration and computational mechanics.

faculty.name: Michael Holst
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Holst's research spans numerical differential equations, mathematical modeling, mathematical physics, and mathematical biology. He develops computational methods for PDEs in applications ranging from biophysics to general relativity. He is a SIAM Fellow and NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Jiawang Nie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in optimization, specializing in polynomial optimization and moment problems.

faculty.name: Bo Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in applied mathematics with work on mathematical biophysics and variational models.

faculty.name: Frederick Manners
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Manners's research centers on arithmetic pseudorandomness -- investigating whether mathematical structures appear random or harbor hidden patterns -- through an in-depth study of Gowers norms and their inverse theory, with applications to combinatorics, number theory, and ergodic theory.

faculty.name: Andrew Suk
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in combinatorics and discrete geometry, including Ramsey-type problems.

faculty.name: Jacques Verstraete
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in extremal and probabilistic combinatorics and graph theory.

faculty.name: Brendon Rhoades
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in algebraic combinatorics and its connections to representation theory.

faculty.name: Steven Sam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor working across commutative algebra, combinatorics, and representation theory.

faculty.name: Alireza Salehi Golsefidy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor working at the interface of number theory, group theory, and expander graphs.

faculty.name: Kristin DeVleming
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in algebraic geometry, working on moduli spaces and compactifications.

faculty.name: Morris Ang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in probability, working on Schramm-Loewner evolution and Liouville quantum gravity.

faculty.name: Ioan Bejenaru
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.sub_field: Automorphic Forms / Langlands Program / Algebraic Geometry
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of automorphic forms, representation theory, and algebraic geometry, with focus on the Langlands program. Fields Medalist (2010) for his proof of the Fundamental Lemma. Current work includes geometrization of the trace formula, Hitchin fibration for higher-dimensional varieties, and Kloosterman sheaves for reductive groups.

faculty.name: Marianna Csörnyei
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: André Neves
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the interplay between topology, geometry, and analysis, using variational methods to study how curvature and topology of ambient spaces influence the existence of stable and unstable critical points of geometric functionals. Elected to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, Simons Investigator.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Brock
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies hyperbolic 3-manifolds and low-dimensional topology; co-proved the Ending Lamination Conjecture; applies geometric methods to complex data analysis.

faculty.name: Ronald Coifman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in nonlinear Fourier analysis and its applications to signal processing, data science, and medical imaging.

faculty.title: Professor

faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Vladimir Rokhlin

faculty.name: Wilhelm Schlag
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Dan Spielman
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Lu Wang

faculty.name: John Wettlaufer
faculty.title: Professor
